title: Data Management and Policies in Cancer Research: Open Access & FAIR Data Principles
author: European Commission
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Presentation material in the  .odp,  .pdf and .pptx format for each individual sessions within the THRIVE Online Training Course:   Data Management and Policies in Cancer Research: Open Access & FAIR Data Principles.     Agenda:  
  Keynote Speaker: Importance of data management in cancer research    Session 1: Principles of Research Data Management (RDM)  • What is Research Data Management? • Why is data management essential for cancer research? • Overview of data lifecycle (collection, storage, sharing, reuse). • Key policies and regulations (e.g., GDPR, national/institutional policies).   Session 2: Open Science, Open Access  • What is Open Science? • Benefits & challenges of Open Access for cancer research.   Session 3: Introduction to FAIR Data Principles    • Institutional support & funding agency requirements (e.g., Horizon Europe). • Breakdown of Each Principle with Examples: • Findable: Unique identifiers (DOI, metadata indexing). • Accessible: Open vs. restricted access, data repositories. • Interoperable: Use of standard vocabularies, formats, ontologies. • Reusable: Clear licensing, rich metadata, provenance tracking. • Metadata standards & best practices. • Experts share real-world examples of FAIR data implementation. • Show a dataset example and ask participants to evaluate: Is this FAIR? • Repositories supporting FAIR data (Zenodo, Dryad, cBioPortal). • FAIR assessment tools (FAIR Metrics, FAIR-Aware).   Session 4: Practical Approaches to Data Sharing & Compliance:   • Data repositories for cancer research (e.g., European Genome-Phenome Archive, cBioPortal). • Licensing & consent: How to legally share patient data. • Idea for group discussion: Challenges participants face in sharing data.
